steps.command: echo "## Git Stats" && echo "Commits (last 30 days): $(git log --since='30 days ago' --oneline | wc -l | tr -d ' ')" && echo "Contributors: $(git log --since='30 days ago' --format='%aN' | sort -u | wc -l | tr -d ' ')" && echo "Branches: $(git branch -a | wc -l | tr -d ' ')" && echo "Latest tag: $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo 'none')"

echo "## Repo Structure" && echo "Top-level dirs:" && ls -d */ 2>/dev/null && echo "---" && echo "Total files: $(find . -type f -not -path './.git/*' -not -path './node_modules/*' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')" && echo "Total dirs: $(find . -type d -not -path './.git/*' -not -path './node_modules/*' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"

steps.instruction: Read the git stats and repo structure data from previous steps. Write a draft project health summary to reports/health-draft.md. Include sections for activity level, structure overview, and any observations (e.g., lots of recent commits = active development).

Read the draft at reports/health-draft.md. Review it for accuracy — cross-check any claims against the raw data in .wheel/outputs/. Fix any inaccuracies, tighten the prose, and write the final version to reports/health-report.md.
